info.description: The deals-web-api manages deals in the phoenix architecture
info.title: Deals Web API

get.description: Lists all accounts that the user has access to based on the user's access token.

Returns a list of audience segments that the specified publisher has access to. This information will give you the corresponding ID for an audience segment. For example, if you want to restrict certain audiences from being targeted in a deal, you will need to specify the audience IDs to exclude when  [creating a new deal](https://api1.indexexchange.com/reference/publisherdealsput).

parameters.description: Replace the <token> placeholder text with your access token. </br>**Example**: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICJRRElHYnh4RkJkWTRucmx50
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description: The publisher account ID associated with the audience segments that you are retrieving. Currently only one account ID is supported. To find the account ID, log in to your Index UI account. The account ID is displayed on the top-left corner of the page under the company name.
parameters.name: legacyAccountID

get.description: Returns a list of all available DSPs that are currently active on Index Exchange (Index) and includes information about the DSPs, such as the DSP ID, which you will need when [creating a new deal](https://api1.indexexchange.com/reference/publisherdealsput).

parameters.description: Replace the <token> placeholder text with your access token. </br>**Example**: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICJRRElHYnh4RkJkWTRucmx50
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description:
The classID of the deal type that you want to return a list of valid DSPs for, where:
'1' = Direct deal
'2' = Platform deal
'3' = Inventory Packages
'4' = Marketplace Packages

If you don't specify a value, a list of all available DSPs that are currently active on Index Exchange is returned.
parameters.name: validForClassID

get.description: Returns all the buyer seats associated with the specified `dspID` and includes information about the buyer seat, such as the buyer ID, which you may need when [creating a new deal](https://api1.indexexchange.com/reference/publisherdealsput).

parameters.description: Replace the <token> placeholder text with your access token. </br>**Example**: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICJRRElHYnh4RkJkWTRucmx50
parameters.name: Authorization

get.description: Returns a list of all publisher-created direct deals that the specified `legacyAccountId` has access to. It also provides details of how this deal is set up, such as which DSP the deal is associated with and what auction type a deal is using.

parameters.description: Replace the <token> placeholder text with your access token. </br>**Example**: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICJRRElHYnh4RkJkWTRucmx50
parameters.name: Authorization

parameters.description: List of auctionTypes to filter the results. Enums(first, fixed, second)
parameters.name: auctionType

parameters.description: The account ID for which you want to see the associated publisher deals. Currently only one account ID is supported. To find the account ID, log in to your Index UI account. The account ID is displayed on the top-left corner of the page under the company name.
parameters.name: legacyAccountId

parameters.description: The number of pages to omit from the result.This field is used along with the `pageSize` field. For example, if `pageSize` is 20 and there are 10 pages in total, you can set `pageOffset` to 5 to display records 101-120 on page five. If you do not specify a `pageOffset`, the `pageOffset` is set to **0** by default.
parameters.name: pageOffset

parameters.description: The maximum number of results that you want to see on a page. By default, all results are displayed on a page.
parameters.name: pageSize

parameters.description: The search string that you want to use to filter the search results. The search string must be part of the internal or external dealID or name associated with the deal. You can enter a full or partial search string.
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: List of statuses to filter the results, where:</br> `A` = Active</br> `E` = Expired</br> `P` = Paused</br> `S` = Stale</br>Note: Deals with a status of `D` (deleted) are not returned. Enums(A, E, P, S)
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: List of deal types to filter the results by, where:</br> `preferred` = This deal type does not compete with open market bids and can be configured to use either a first or fixed price auction.</br> `custom` = This deal type allows for custom deal configuration settings.</br> `private` = This deal type is configured to use a first-price auction and to compete with open market bids.</br> `programmaticGuaranteed` = A programmatic guaranteed deal is made between a publisher and a buyer with specific terms and pricing for inventory that is reserved by the publisher for the buyer. Enums(preferred, custom, private, programmaticGuaranteed)
parameters.name: dealType

parameters.description: The sort method to receive the deals data, where:</br>`auctionType` = sort results alphabetically by the auctionType associated with the deals.</br>`dealID` = sort results alphabetically by the externalDealID associated with the deals.</br>`dealName` = sort results alphabetically by the dealName associated with the deals.</br>`dealType` = sort results by the deal type associated with the deals.</br>`dspName` = sort results alphabetically by the dspName associated with the deals.</br>`endDate` = sort results by the endDate associated with the deals.</br>`externalDealID` = sort results alphabetically by the externalDealID associated with the deals.</br>`internalDealID` = sort results numerically by the internalDealID associated with the deals.</br>`rate` = sort results numerically by the rate associated with the deals.</br>`startDate` = sort results by the startDate associated with the deals.</br>`status` = sort results alphabetically by the status associated with the deals.
parameters.name: sortField

parameters.description: The sort order for the specified sort method, where:</br> `asc` = order the results in ascending order.</br> `desc` = order the results in descending order.
parameters.name: sortOrder

post.description:
Creates a new deal for the following deal types:
  • Inventory Package: An Index-created deal that allows buyers to purchase publisher inventory using open auction mechanics using a single deal ID, and does not require any negotiation between a buyer and publisher
  • Marketplace Package: A Marketplace Partner-created deal negotiated with a buyer where they bundle inventory from multiple publishers, add targeting, and price them according to the value they bring to the buyer using a single deal ID.
  • Deal with Marketplaces: A publisher-created deal negotiated between a publisher and a Marketplace Partner, usually comprising a sub-set of the publisher's inventory that can be targeted by the Marketplace Partner in a Marketplace Package. This deal cannot transact unless it is included in a Marketplace Package.
    • .

requestBody.description: A list of operations indicating the values to update for a deal

id.description: The unique ID assigned to the publisher account.

accountID.description: The accountID of the deal owner.

classID.description:
The class ID for the deal, where:
`3` = Inventory Package
`4` = Marketplace Package
`5` = Deal with Marketplaces

endDate.description: The end date for the deal in YYYY-MM-DD format.

externalDealID.description: A unique deal ID string that was created for this deal. This is the descriptive identifier and not the internal database identifier specified in the `internalDealID`.

floor.description: The minimum acceptable net price (floor) for a winning bid for a piece of inventory.

internalDealID.description: The internal Index system-generated deal identifier. This is the internal database identifier and not the descriptive `externalDealID`.

modifiedDate.description: The last modified date and time this deal was updated in YYYY-MM-DD'T'HH:mm:ss'Z' format.

startDate.description: The start date for the deal in YYYY-MM-DD format. The date cannot be earlier than the current date for which you are creating the deal.

status.description:
The status of the deal, where:
`A` = active
`P` = paused

For example, if you prefer this deal to win over another deal you've created, set the priority of this deal to 10 and the priority of the other deal to a lower number.
